description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and narrowing at the top and bottom. The barrel is constructed from vertical wooden staves, held tightly together by four robust black metal hoops, two near the top and two near the bottom. The wood has a natural, weathered brown finish, and the top surface features a faint circular pattern, possibly a logo or branding. On one side, there is a small wooden bung, used for filling or emptying the barrel. One side of the barrel displays white stenciled text reading 'PORTO CALEM - EST. 1756', indicating it may have been used for storing or transporting port wine. The overall appearance suggests durability and a design suited for storage in urban or industrial environments.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with black metal hoops and white stenciled text, featuring a small wooden bung and a circular branded top.
description_view_0: The barrel's front shows the wooden bung and the texture of the wood, with metal hoops clearly visible.
description_view_1: This view highlights the white stenciled 'PORTO CALEM' text and branding, making it the most informative side.
description_view_2: The side view shows the plain wooden staves and metal hoops, with no visible markings or bung.
description_view_3: Another plain side of the barrel, showing the curvature and metal hoops, with a hint of the bung visible at the edge.
